President Donald Trump holds his 9th cabinet meeting at the White House.

byDaniel Zivian

CHEBOYGAN COUNTY, Mich., (WPBN/WGTU) -- Opening ceremonies for Olympics took place Friday in Paris, and a member of the U.S. Olympic wrestling team has a connection to northern Michigan.

Two American flags are planted in the street and a big one flies by the door.

That's the front porch of Wayne and Cherie Precord in Cheboygan.

But it's not idle patriotism.

Inside, they'll watch their grandson Adam Coon wrestle for Team USA.

"It’s been Adam’s dream since he was a little boy," said Cherie Precord. "He was so big, even at that age, so tall. Just a big kid. My daughter, Dana, had to carry his birth certificate with her a lot of times, just to prove that he was the same age as the kids that were wrestling."

The Precords have seen him wrestle at the University of Michigan and in his hometown of Folwerville, Michigan.

They were in Folwerville this month as he rode in the Fourth of July parade.

There's plenty to brag about: the Precords said Adam is a married man and has a master's degree in aerospace engineering.

"I'm really proud of him because he has been such a good kid growing up. He worked hard with his dad and he’s been so good with kids. We are very proud of that young man," said Wayne Precord.

But Wayne and Cherie won't be joining the family in Paris, due in part to health issues.

Thankfully, Cherie said the community is ecstatic.

"They love it. I mean, it’s been on the radio, it was in the paper. They almost feel like they know this kid because he’s related to us," Cherie said. "Tons of people have called and asked, ‘What day is he wrestling? What time?’"

Adam Coon is wrestling in the 130-kilogram weight, which is about 285 pounds.

He'll be competing on August 5, and if he advances, on August 6.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: 'Very proud of that young man': Cheboygan couple to watch grandson in the Olympics
description: Opening ceremonies for Olympics took place Friday in Paris, and a member of the U.S. Olympic wrestling team has a connection to northern Michigan.
